name: Gravel route through the Sierra de Cardeña Natural Park
name_original: Ruta gravel por el parque natural Sierra de Cardeña
description:
The dehesa is the most extensive landscape in Spain and the holm oak is the tree with the most specimens in the country. In this ecosystem, which covers a large part of Andalusia and Extremadura, Iberian pigs roam in search of their favorite food, the acorn. In addition to a wide variety of traditional agricultural farms, Iberian lynxes, wild boars, roe deer, deer, foxes and rabbits run through the pasture.

With this Route through the Sierra de Cardeña natural park, we want to offer you a brief immersion in its postcard landscape and its country culture. The itinerary is circular from the town of Cardeña, where you can reach by road, and passes through two other ideal towns to stop and enjoy local specialties, mainly Iberian pork products, in addition to contemplating its traditional architecture of whitewashed houses. .

The itinerary runs along dirt tracks between holm oaks, cork oaks and oak groves under whose shade aromatic bushes proliferate. Apart from the mild temperatures of winter, you can cycle through Córdoba in spring and autumn, each season offers you its benefits. Remember to bring enough water and food, as well as some spare parts and some sunscreen to enjoy riding under the warm Andalusian sun.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Cardeña offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park, characterized by rolling hills and low mountains. The landscape features extensive Mediterranean woodlands of holm oaks and cork oaks, alongside dehesa, an open agro-forestry system. Ancient granite outcrops and river valleys, including the River Yeguas, create varied riding conditions with both challenging climbs and scenic trails. This region provides a rich natural environment for gravel cyclists. Best gravel bike trails around Cardeña The most popular gravel bike trail is Gravel route through the Sierra de Cardeña Natural Park, a 25.1 miles (40.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route navigates through the heart of the natural park, offering varied surfaces and elevation changes. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna – Dehesa de Los Pedroches loop from Villanueva de Córdoba, a difficult 61.4 miles (98.9 km) path. This extensive loop traverses dehesa landscapes and offers views of traditional hamlets. Local gravel bikers also love the Sanctuary of the Virgin – Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna loop from Villanueva de Córdoba-Los Pedroches, a 70.5 miles (113.5 km) trail leading through Mediterranean woodlands and open pastures, often completed in about 10 hours 21 minutes. Gravel biking around Cardeña is defined by Mediterranean woodlands, dehesa, and granite outcrops, with routes that offer both challenging climbs and scenic paths. The network provides options for experienced gravel bikers seeking longer, more demanding rides. The routes in Cardeña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 1 review. More than 30 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Cardeña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Cardeña?
A: The gravel bike trails in Cardeña traverse a diverse landscape of rolling hills and low mountains within the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park. You'll encounter extensive Mediterranean woodlands with holm oaks and cork oaks, as well as open dehesa, a traditional agro-forestry system. The routes also feature ancient granite outcrops and follow river valleys like the River Yeguas, offering varied surfaces and elevation changes.

Q: Are there challenging gravel bike routes in Cardeña?
A: Yes, the gravel biking routes in Cardeña are generally considered challenging. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1502561290/gravel-route-through-the-sierra-de-cardena-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel route through the Sierra de Cardeña Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is rated difficult, as are other popular loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna – Dehesa de Los Pedroches loop from Villanueva de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 100 km.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while gravel biking in Cardeña?
A: Cardeña's routes offer stunning natural beauty. You can expect to see ancient granite outcrops, lush riparian vegetation along rivers like the Yeguas, and extensive Mediterranean woodlands. Notable natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polar Viewpoint – Aldea del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt;, which is also known for stargazing.

Q: Are there any historical sites along the gravel routes in Cardeña?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. You might encounter remains from the Neolithic period, such as megalithic tombs, and sites from Iberian and Roman times. Specific historical highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Batanera Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3169663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaescrita Cave Paintings&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Cardeña?
A: Given the Mediterranean climate, the best times for gravel biking in Cardeña are typically spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, making for more comfortable riding conditions to explore the natural park's diverse landscapes and wildlife.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while gravel biking in Cardeña?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park is a haven for Iberian fauna. Cyclists often encounter deer, wild boar, and various raptors like golden and imperial eagles. It's also one of the last refuges for the Iberian lynx and wolves, making wildlife spotting a potential highlight of your ride.

Q: Are there any loop routes for gravel biking in Cardeña?
A: Yes, there are several excellent loop routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin – Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna loop from Villanueva de Córdoba-Los Pedroches&lt;/a&gt;, which is a long and rewarding ride through Mediterranean woodlands and open pastures.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about gravel biking in Cardeña?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the immersive experience within the natural park, and the challenging yet scenic paths that showcase the region's unique Mediterranean woodlands and granite formations.

Q: Are there visitor centers or information points in the Cardeña area?
A: Yes, the Venta Nueva Visitor Centre, located just outside Cardeña, provides valuable information about the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park. It features audiovisual shows and exhibitions focusing on the Iberian lynx and the Mediterranean woodland ecosystem, and has a recreational area for resting.

Q: Where can I find accommodation or places to eat near the gravel trails?
A: The former agricultural hamlet of Aldea del Cerezo offers rehabilitated stone cottages for rural accommodation. The villages themselves, like Cardeña and Montoro, are known for their local cuisine, including Iberian acorn-fed ham and fried suckling pig, providing options for meals and refreshments.

Q: How long are the typical gravel bike routes in Cardeña?
A: The gravel routes in Cardeña vary in length, catering to experienced riders seeking longer distances. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1502561290/gravel-route-through-the-sierra-de-cardena-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel route through the Sierra de Cardeña Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is about 40 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin – Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna loop from Villanueva de Córdoba-Los Pedroches&lt;/a&gt; extends to over 113 km.

Q: Is there a specific viewpoint for birdwatching in the area?
A: Yes, there is a significant bird viewpoint located at the Tejoneras and Dehesa Yeguas reservoirs. This spot is a crucial resting place for various migratory birds, including herons, egrets, and grebes, offering excellent opportunities for birdwatching during your visit.
tips: In the center of the town of Cardeña there is the town hall, a square with a fountain, a store selling Iberian products from the area, and some bars with the gastronomic specialties of the region. The Iberian acorn-fed ham and the fried suckling pig are true gems that you must try.
Parallel to the road, a very beautiful path runs between oaks and pines.

This track is in very good condition and runs through a beautiful landscape of pastures and oak forests. In the higher areas, a charming view spreads over the foliage. If you are lucky you will see herds of Iberian pigs in search of acorns.
It is a magnificent place to rest and, at night, see the stars in the heart of the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro natural park.
